hero.title: A new vision for shelter.
hero.summary: Everyone Deserves Housing brings outreach, shelter, healthcare, behavioral health, and housing pathways into one practical, more human system.
highlights: Triage into the right shelter and housing pathway

mission: Care should feel immediate, coordinated, and human.
rationale: People living unsheltered need immediate, on-site access to care rather than referral-only pathways.
Behavioral health and substance use treatment are more effective when they are co-located and continuous.
Shelter environments can re-trigger trauma and weaken engagement.
Staff experience secondary trauma and need safer, more sustainable environments and workflows.

guidingPrinciples.title: Dignity and belonging
guidingPrinciples.text: Spaces and services should feel respectful, safe, and genuinely welcoming.

guidingPrinciples.title: Care where people are
guidingPrinciples.text: Street-level homelessness calls for immediate support, not a chain of referrals.

guidingPrinciples.title: Integrated response
guidingPrinciples.text: Shelter, healthcare, behavioral health, and housing pathways work better together.

guidingPrinciples.title: Equity in design and outcomes
guidingPrinciples.text: The model should work for people most affected by poverty, trauma, and exclusion.

programElements.title: Open door
programElements.text: 24/7 access, basic needs, and immediate stabilization without preconditions.

programElements.title: Care on site
programElements.text: Medical, behavioral health, and substance use support are part of the core response.

programElements.title: Recovery pathways
programElements.text: Support people across a full continuum instead of forcing one fixed path.

programElements.title: Trauma-informed spaces
programElements.text: Design the environment and the workflow to support both clients and staff.

programElements.title: Faster housing moves
programElements.text: Use triage and coordinated planning to connect people to the right next step sooner.

pathways.intro: The model starts with one stabilization hub for assessment and triage, then routes each person into the tailored shelter or housing path that best fits their needs.

hub.title: Stabilization hub
hub.text: A short-term front door for immediate basic needs, shelter, assessment, and multidisciplinary triage before placement.

services.title: Medical respite
services.text: A shelter setting for people who need a safer place to recover and stabilize while receiving coordinated medical support.

services.title: Addiction recovery
services.text: A recovery-oriented shelter path with substance use and behavioral health support built into the care model.

services.title: Low barrier
services.text: A low-barrier shelter option that reduces entry requirements and keeps access immediate, practical, and welcoming.

services.title: Senior supportive
services.text: A senior-focused shelter setting with supportive and palliative care capacity for older adults with higher daily needs.

services.title: Rapid rehousing
services.text: A scattered-site housing pathway for people who can quickly regain stability with coordinated transition support.

designPrinciples.title: Nested layers
designPrinciples.text: People need choices around privacy, noise, and social connection.

designPrinciples.title: Identity anchors
designPrinciples.text: The setting should reflect community, culture, and personal dignity.

designPrinciples.title: Workforce sustainability
designPrinciples.text: Staff support is part of care quality, not an extra.

partnerships: COPES assessments and home care services
outcomes: Better alignment between care setting and client need
startupPlan: Secure a fiscal sponsor for 6 to 12 months.
Form the nonprofit and complete core legal setup.
Develop 2 to 3 enhanced shelters within 18 months of startup.
